--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fd80
+title: Remove "Delete Attachment" from task context menu — fold into cross-cutting `entity.delete`
+---
+## What
+
+Right-clicking a task currently offers a "Delete Attachment" entry. Attachments are stored as a multi-value field on a task, so asking to "delete the attachment" when there could be zero or many makes no sense — the only coherent place for that action is a context menu fired on a specific attachment chip/tile.
+
+**Root cause** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/attachment.yaml:11-22`): the `attachment.delete` command is scoped `scope: "entity:task"` with both `task_id` and `id` coming `from: args`. The `entity:task` scope pin means the cross-cutting emitter surfaces it on every task moniker, producing the user-visible "Delete Attachment" row. Worse, the context menu dispatcher can't supply `task_id`/`id` as args, so clicking that row fails silently or with a confusing error.
+
+**Latent second bug surfaced by the same research**: the cross-cutting `entity.delete` command (`entity.yaml:71-77`) already emits for attachment targets (see `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_surface_matrix.rs:1381-1406`), but `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:125-146`) has no match arm for `entity_type == "attachment"`, so it returns `"unknown entity type for delete: attachment"`. Right-clicking an attachment chip today picks either the broken `entity.delete` or the broken `attachment.delete` — both fail.
+
+**Fix approach — unify on `entity.delete`** (aligns with the commands refactor that already consolidated cut/copy/paste/delete/archive into cross-cutting commands):
+
+1. Delete `attachment.delete` from `attachment.yaml` entirely (lines 11-22).
+2. Delete `AttachmentDeleteCmd` struct and its impl in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:372-393`.
+3. Remove the registration `map.insert("attachment.delete".into(), Arc::new(entity_commands::AttachmentDeleteCmd))` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:100-101`.
+4. Add an `"attachment"` match arm to `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` at `entity_commands.rs:135-145`:
+   ```rust
+   "attachment" => {
+       let task_id = ctx
+           .resolve_entity_id("task")
+           .ok_or_else(|| CommandError::ExecutionFailed(
+               "attachment delete requires a task in scope".into(),
+           ))?;
+       run_op(&crate::attachment::DeleteAttachment::new(task_id, id), &kanban).await
+   }
+   ```
+   The `task_id` comes from the scope chain — `ctx.resolve_entity_id("task")` walks innermost-first. When the user right-clicks an attachment chip inside a task, the scope chain is `["attachment:<id>", "task:<id>", "column:<id>", ...]`, so this resolves correctly. The attachment `id` comes from the target moniker already parsed at line 133.
+
+**Why this over just changing `attachment.delete`'s scope**: Keeping `attachment.delete` as a parallel path would leave two broken delete paths for attachments (the cross-cutting `entity.delete` still wouldn't work on attachments unless we also fix it). Unifying fixes both at once and removes a near-duplicate command. Aligns with the project's explicit command-organization rule (`entity.yaml:26-42`): cross-cutting destructive actions live in `entity.yaml`; per-type files hold only type-specific commands.
+
+**What remains in `attachment.yaml`**: `attachment.open` and `attachment.reveal` stay — they're truly type-specific (open file in OS default app, reveal in Finder). Their `scope: "attachment"` pin correctly confines them to attachment context menus only.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Right-clicking a task does NOT offer "Delete Attachment" anywhere in the context menu.
+- [ ] Right-clicking an attachment chip DOES offer a single "Delete Attachment" entry (emitted as `entity.delete` with the attachment target, rendered via the `"Delete {{entity.type}}"` template in `entity.yaml:72`).
+- [ ] Clicking that entry successfully removes the attachment from the parent task on disk and fires the appropriate `entity-removed` / `entity-field-changed` event sequence (existing `DeleteAttachment` operation is unchanged).
+- [ ] Undo restores the attachment (since `entity.delete` is `undoable: true` per `entity.yaml:73` and `DeleteAttachment` is undoable).
+- [ ] `attachment.open` and `attachment.reveal` are unaffected — they still appear on attachment context menus.
+- [ ] No other entity type's context menu changes (task/tag/column/actor/project delete paths still work via their existing match arms in `DeleteEntityCmd::execute`).
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Update `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_surface_matrix.rs::matrix_attachment_delete_surface_emits_but_attachment_delete_present` (line 1381-1406):
+  - Rename to `matrix_attachment_delete_surface_emits_entity_delete_only`.
+  - Keep the assertion that `entity.delete` is present with target `attachment:/tmp/x.png` and rendered name `"Delete Attachment"`.
+  - REMOVE the assertion that `attachment.delete` is in the surface (lines 1400-1405). Replace with an assertion that NO command with id `attachment.delete` is in the surface.
+- [ ] Update the module comment at `command_surface_matrix.rs:1344-1349` to drop the reference to "type-specific delete (`attachment.delete`)" and note that the cross-cutting `entity.delete` now handles attachments.
+- [ ] New Rust test `delete_entity_deletes_attachment_via_scope_chain` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs` tests module:
+  1. Set up a task with one attachment.
+  2. Build `CommandContext` with target `"attachment:<id>"` and scope chain `["attachment:<id>", "task:<taskid>", "column:todo"]`.
+  3. Execute `DeleteEntityCmd`. Assert no error, attachment list on the task is now empty.
+- [ ] New Rust test `delete_entity_attachment_missing_task_in_scope_errors`:
+  1. Same target, but scope chain has NO `task:` moniker.
+  2. Execute `DeleteEntityCmd`. Assert `CommandError::ExecutionFailed` with the "requires a task in scope" message.
+- [ ] New surface test `task_context_menu_does_not_include_delete_attachment` in `command_surface_matrix.rs`:
+  1. Build scope chain `["task:01X", "column:todo"]` (NO attachment moniker).
+  2. Assert no resolved command has id `attachment.delete`.
+  3. Assert `entity.delete` is present with target `task:01X` and rendered name `"Delete Task"` (not "Delete Attachment").
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban command_surface_matrix` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban entity_commands` — all passing.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd`. Start by writing `task_context_menu_does_not_include_delete_attachment` — the failing test reproduces the user's complaint. Then delete the YAML entry, delete the Rust struct + registration, add the match arm, and update/rename the existing surface-matrix test. The `delete_entity_deletes_attachment_via_scope_chain` test validates the new behavior end-to-end.
+- Do NOT touch `attachment.open`, `attachment.reveal`, `DeleteAttachment` operation, or any dispatch rewriting in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs`.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What

Right-clicking a task currently offers a "Delete Attachment" entry. Attachments are stored as a multi-value field on a task, so asking to "delete the attachment" when there could be zero or many makes no sense — the only coherent place for that action is a context menu fired on a specific attachment chip/tile.

**Root cause** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/attachment.yaml:11-22`): the `attachment.delete` command is scoped `scope: "entity:task"` with both `task_id` and `id` coming `from: args`. The `entity:task` scope pin means the cross-cutting emitter surfaces it on every task moniker, producing the user-visible "Delete Attachment" row. Worse, the context menu dispatcher can't supply `task_id`/`id` as args, so clicking that row fails silently or with a confusing error.

**Latent second bug surfaced by the same research**: the cross-cutting `entity.delete` command (`entity.yaml:71-77`) already emits for attachment targets (see `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_surface_matrix.rs:1381-1406`), but `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:125-146`) has no match arm for `entity_type == "attachment"`, so it returns `"unknown entity type for delete: attachment"`. Right-clicking an attachment chip today picks either the broken `entity.delete` or the broken `attachment.delete` — both fail.

**Fix approach — unify on `entity.delete`** (aligns with the commands refactor that already consolidated cut/copy/paste/delete/archive into cross-cutting commands):

1. Delete `attachment.delete` from `attachment.yaml` entirely (lines 11-22).
2. Delete `AttachmentDeleteCmd` struct and its impl in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:372-393`.
3. Remove the registration `map.insert("attachment.delete".into(), Arc::new(entity_commands::AttachmentDeleteCmd))` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:100-101`.
4. Add an `"attachment"` match arm to `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` at `entity_commands.rs:135-145`:
   ```rust
   "attachment" => {
       let task_id = ctx
           .resolve_entity_id("task")
           .ok_or_else(|| CommandError::ExecutionFailed(
               "attachment delete requires a task in scope".into(),
           ))?;
       run_op(&crate::attachment::DeleteAttachment::new(task_id, id), &kanban).await
   }
   ```
   The `task_id` comes from the scope chain — `ctx.resolve_entity_id("task")` walks innermost-first. When the user right-clicks an attachment chip inside a task, the scope chain is `["attachment:<id>", "task:<id>", "column:<id>", ...]`, so this resolves correctly. The attachment `id` comes from the target moniker already parsed at line 133.

**Why this over just changing `attachment.delete`'s scope**: Keeping `attachment.delete` as a parallel path would leave two broken delete paths for attachments (the cross-cutting `entity.delete` still wouldn't work on attachments unless we also fix it). Unifying fixes both at once and removes a near-duplicate command. Aligns with the project's explicit command-organization rule (`entity.yaml:26-42`): cross-cutting destructive actions live in `entity.yaml`; per-type files hold only type-specific commands.

**What remains in `attachment.yaml`**: `attachment.open` and `attachment.reveal` stay — they're truly type-specific (open file in OS default app, reveal in Finder). Their `scope: "attachment"` pin correctly confines them to attachment context menus only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Right-clicking a task does NOT offer "Delete Attachment" anywhere in the context menu.
- [ ] Right-clicking an attachment chip DOES offer a single "Delete Attachment" entry (emitted as `entity.delete` with the attachment target, rendered via the `"Delete {{entity.type}}"` template in `entity.yaml:72`).
- [ ] Clicking that entry successfully removes the attachment from the parent task on disk and fires the appropriate `entity-removed` / `entity-field-changed` event sequence (existing `DeleteAttachment` operation is unchanged).
- [ ] Undo restores the attachment (since `entity.delete` is `undoable: true` per `entity.yaml:73` and `DeleteAttachment` is undoable).
- [ ] `attachment.open` and `attachment.reveal` are unaffected — they still appear on attachment context menus.
- [ ] No other entity type's context menu changes (task/tag/column/actor/project delete paths still work via their existing match arms in `DeleteEntityCmd::execute`).

## Tests

- [ ] Update `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_surface_matrix.rs::matrix_attachment_delete_surface_emits_but_attachment_delete_present` (line 1381-1406):
  - Rename to `matrix_attachment_delete_surface_emits_entity_delete_only`.
  - Keep the assertion that `entity.delete` is present with target `attachment:/tmp/x.png` and rendered name `"Delete Attachment"`.
  - REMOVE the assertion that `attachment.delete` is in the surface (lines 1400-1405). Replace with an assertion that NO command with id `attachment.delete` is in the surface.
- [ ] Update the module comment at `command_surface_matrix.rs:1344-1349` to drop the reference to "type-specific delete (`attachment.delete`)" and note that the cross-cutting `entity.delete` now handles attachments.
- [ ] New Rust test `delete_entity_deletes_attachment_via_scope_chain` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs` tests module:
  1. Set up a task with one attachment.
  2. Build `CommandContext` with target `"attachment:<id>"` and scope chain `["attachment:<id>", "task:<taskid>", "column:todo"]`.
  3. Execute `DeleteEntityCmd`. Assert no error, attachment list on the task is now empty.
- [ ] New Rust test `delete_entity_attachment_missing_task_in_scope_errors`:
  1. Same target, but scope chain has NO `task:` moniker.
  2. Execute `DeleteEntityCmd`. Assert `CommandError::ExecutionFailed` with the "requires a task in scope" message.
- [ ] New surface test `task_context_menu_does_not_include_delete_attachment` in `command_surface_matrix.rs`:
  1. Build scope chain `["task:01X", "column:todo"]` (NO attachment moniker).
  2. Assert no resolved command has id `attachment.delete`.
  3. Assert `entity.delete` is present with target `task:01X` and rendered name `"Delete Task"` (not "Delete Attachment").
- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban command_surface_matrix` and `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban entity_commands` — all passing.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd`. Start by writing `task_context_menu_does_not_include_delete_attachment` — the failing test reproduces the user's complaint. Then delete the YAML entry, delete the Rust struct + registration, add the match arm, and update/rename the existing surface-matrix test. The `delete_entity_deletes_attachment_via_scope_chain` test validates the new behavior end-to-end.
- Do NOT touch `attachment.open`, `attachment.reveal`, `DeleteAttachment` operation, or any dispatch rewriting in `kanban-app/src/commands.rs`.

 Right-clicking a task currently offers a "Delete Attachment" entry. Attachments are stored as a multi-value field on a task, so asking to "delete the attachment" when there could be zero or many makes no sense — the only coherent place for that action is a context menu fired on a specific attachment chip/tile.

-**Root cause** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/attachment.yaml:11-22`): the `attachment.delete` command is scoped `scope: "entity:task"` with both `task_id` and `id` coming `from: args`. The `entity:task` scope pin means the cross-cutting emitter surfaces it on every task moniker, producing the user-visible "Delete Attachment" row. Worse, the context menu dispatcher can't supply `task_id`/`id` as args, so clicking that row fails silently or with a confusing error.
-
-**Latent second bug surfaced by the same research**: the cross-cutting `entity.delete` command (`entity.yaml:71-77`) already emits for attachment targets (see `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/command_surface_matrix.rs:1381-1406`), but `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:125-146`) has no match arm for `entity_type == "attachment"`, so it returns `"unknown entity type for delete: attachment"`. Right-clicking an attachment chip today picks either the broken `entity.delete` or the broken `attachment.delete` — both fail.
-
-**Fix approach — unify on `entity.delete`** (aligns with the commands refactor that already consolidated cut/copy/paste/delete/archive into cross-cutting commands):
-
-1. Delete `attachment.delete` from `attachment.yaml` entirely (lines 11-22).
-2. Delete `AttachmentDeleteCmd` struct and its impl in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/entity_commands.rs:372-393`.
-3. Remove the registration `map.insert("attachment.delete".into(), Arc::new(entity_commands::AttachmentDeleteCmd))` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/mod.rs:100-101`.
-4. Add an `"attachment"` match arm to `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` at `entity_commands.rs:135-145`:
-   ```rust
-   "attachment" => {
-       let task_id = ctx
-           .resolve_entity_id("task")
-           .ok_or_else(|| CommandError::ExecutionFailed(
-               "attachment delete requires a task in scope".into(),
-           ))?;
-       run_op(&crate::attachment::DeleteAttachment::new(task_id, id), &kanban).await
-   }
-   ```
-   The `task_id` comes from the scope chain — `ctx.resolve_entity_id("task")` walks innermost-first. When the user right-clicks an attachment chip inside a task, the scope chain is `["attachment:<id>", "task:<id>", "column:<id>", ...]`, so this resolves correctly. The attachment `id` comes from the target moniker already parsed at line 133.
-
-**Why this over just changing `attachment.delete`'s scope**: Keeping `attachment.delete` as a parallel path would leave two broken delete paths for attachments (the cross-cutting `entity.delete` still wouldn't work on attachments unless we also fix it). Unifying fixes both at once and removes a near-duplicate command. Aligns with the project's explicit command-organization rule (`entity.yaml:26-42`): cross-cutting destructive actions live in `entity.yaml`; per-type files hold only type-specific commands.
+**Root cause** (`swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/attachment.yaml:11-22`): the `attachment.delete` command is scoped `scope: "entity:task"` with both `task_id` and `id` coming `from: args`. The `entity:task` scope pin means the cross-cutting emitter surfaces it on every task moniker, producing the user-visible "Delete Attachment" row.

-**What remains in `attachment.yaml`**: `attachment.open` and `attachment.reveal` stay — they're truly type-specific (open file in OS default app, reveal in Finder). Their `scope: "attachment"` pin correctly confines them to attachment context menus only.
+**Latent second bug surfaced by the same research**: the cross-cutting `entity.delete` command already emits for attachment targets, but `DeleteEntityCmd::execute` had no match arm for `entity_type == "attachment"`.

+**Third latent bug uncovered during TDD**: `DeleteAttachment::execute` used `task.get_string_list("attachments").contains(&self.id)` on an already-enriched attachments field (array of objects, not strings) — so the check always returned false and the operation was never actually working. Confirmed with user, fixed as part of this card.
